f1Justification: Ransomware attacks increased 150% in financial sector (FS-ISAC 2025). Weekly phishing attempts observed
f2Justification: Email security gateway 85% effective. EDR deployed but not tuned. User awareness training quarterly
f3Justification: 23 medium vulnerabilities in email infrastructure. Phishing simulation 18% click rate
f4Justification: No ransomware incidents in 24 months. 2 contained phishing attempts
f5Justification: 450 endpoints. 12 internet-facing services. Standard attack surface for size
f6Justification: NIS2 requirements increasing. Sector-wide targeting by ransomware groups

f1Justification: Vulnerability exploitation less common than phishing but increasing. Average 15-day patch window
f2Justification: Automated patching 90% coverage. WAF in place. Vulnerability scanning weekly
f3Justification: 8 critical CVEs in last quarter. Patch deployment avg 12 days
f4Justification: No exploitation incidents. Good patching track record
f6Justification: Standard regulatory environment. No specific targeting indicators

f1Justification: SQL injection attempts daily. Financial data highly targeted
f2Justification: WAF deployed. Code review process. SAST/DAST in pipeline
f3Justification: Customer portal last pentest found 3 medium SQLi vectors
f4Justification: No successful SQLi in 36 months. Attempts blocked by WAF
f5Justification: 1 customer portal. 500K customer records. Standard exposure
f6Justification: GDPR enforcement intensifying. €1.2B fines issued sector-wide 2024

f1Justification: Insider threats underreported. HR data valuable on dark web
f2Justification: DLP deployed. Access logging. Background checks standard
f3Justification: Excessive access rights identified in last audit. 15 users with unnecessary HR access
f4Justification: 1 minor insider incident 18 months ago. Policy violation no data loss
f5Justification: HR system isolated. Limited integration points

f1Justification: Credential stuffing attacks continuous. 10M+ credentials leaked monthly globally
f2Justification: MFA 80% deployed. Password policy strong. No credential monitoring
f3Justification: Good password hygiene. MFA gaps in legacy systems
f4Justification: 3 credential stuffing attempts in last 6 months. All blocked by MFA
f5Justification: Customer portal + employee portal. 50K active accounts

f1Justification: Spear-phishing targeting executives increasing. Privileged accounts high value
f2Justification: PAM deployed. Admin MFA enforced. Limited admin accounts
f5Justification: 12 admin accounts. Segmented network. Limited blast radius

f1Justification: SaaS breaches increasing 40% YoY. Multi-tenant risks
f2Justification: Vendor risk assessments. Contractual requirements. Limited visibility
f3Justification: 3 critical vendors lack SOC2. Security questionnaire gaps identified
f4Justification: 1 vendor incident 24 months ago. Minimal impact to us
f5Justification: 45 SaaS vendors. 12 critical. Data in vendor systems
f6Justification: DORA third-party requirements. NIS2 supply chain focus

f1Justification: Natural disasters rare in region. Infrastructure failures uncommon
f2Justification: DR site operational. Daily backups. Annual DR test passed
f3Justification: Single data center dependency. Generator tested monthly
f5Justification: 1 primary DC. 1 DR site. Cloud backup
